content.claim: A Finnish difference-in-differences study found that after school closure, population in surrounding communities decreased more than before closure, but this evidence is from a different administrative and funding context and is not directly validated for Hungary.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Implementation planning lens
limitations: Evidence is from Finland, not Hungary; causal mechanisms may differ under different governance and incentive structures
Directionality of causation (closure causes depopulation vs. depopulation causes closure) is not distinguished in the note
No Hungarian domestic causal study of this relationship was located in the search
Applicability to Hungarian context where schools are centrally funded and staffed differs from Finnish context
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
